title: Which Function Do Insider Threat Programs Aim To Fulfill
source_filename: Which Function Do Insider Threat Programs Aim To Fulfill.docx

qas.q: you find information that you know to be classified on the internet what should you do?
qas.a: Note the website URL and report the situation to your security point of contact

qas.q: which of the following is ture of unclassified information?
qas.a: it could affect the safety of government personnel, missions, and systems

qas.q: which of the following is a security best practice for protecting personally identifiable information (PII) ?
qas.a: Encrypt and digitally sign e-mails containing PII when sending them to non-Government accounts.

qas.q: which designation marks information that does not have potential to damage national security?
qas.a: Unclassified.

qas.q: which of the following is NOT an example of CUI?
qas.a: Press release data.

qas.q: which of the following is NOT a correct way to protect CUI?
qas.a: CUI may be stored on any password protected system.

qas.q: which of the following is NOT a type of malicious code?
qas.a: software that installs itself without the users knowledge.

qas.q: which of the following is an example of malicious code?
qas.a: all of these.

qas.q: how can malicious code cause damage?
qas.a: do not access website links in email messages.

qas.q: how can you avoid downloading malicious code?
qas.a: Exceptionally grave damage

qas.q: which of the following is a good practice for telework?
qas.a: you must have your organizations permission to telework.

qas.q: what is the basis for the handling and storage of classified data?
qas.a: classified material must be appropriately marked.

qas.q: what level of damage to national security can you reasonably expect top secret information to cause if disclosed?
qas.a: proactively identify potential threats and formulate holistic mitigation response.

qas.q: which of the following is true about telework?
qas.a: unusual interest in classified information.

qas.q: which of the following is true of protecting classified data?
qas.a: 3 or more indicators.

qas.q: what function do insider threat programs aim to fulfill?
qas.a: ???actively seek content that confirms the information???

qas.q: which of the following is a potential insider threat indicator?
qas.a: ???deleting post containing personal information on a regular basis???

qas.q: based on the description that follows, how many potential insider threat indicator(s) are displayed? a colleague abruptly becomes hostile and unpleasant after previously enjoying positive working relationships with peers, purchases and unusually expensive new car and has unexplained absences from work.
qas.a: ???actively engage in non professional and non routine contacts with foreign nationals.???

qas.q: a trusted friend in your social network post a link to a vaccine information on a website known to you. what action should you take?
qas.a: CPCON 1

qas.q: ow can you protect yourself on social networking sites?
qas.a: remove his CAC and lock his work station.

qas.q: how can you protect your organization on social networking sites?
qas.a: decline to let the person in and redirect her to security.

qas.q: which cyber protection condition (CPCON) establishes a protection priority focus on critical functions only?
qas.a: a common access card and personal identification number.

qas.q:
what should the employee do differently?
Incident#1
qas.a: you should remove and take you CAC/PIV card whenever you leave your workstation.

qas.q:
what should the employee do differently?
Incident#2
qas.a: Confirm the individuals need to know and access.

qas.q: which of the following is an example of two- factor authentication?
qas.a: the provide guidance on reasons for and duration of classification of information

qas.q: which of the following is true of the (CAC) or (PIV) card?
qas.a:
(THIS IS A POTENTIAL)
the one not on this list is the answer
-SHUT DOWN OF SYSTEMS
-COMPROMISE OF INFORMATION, SYSTEMS, PROGRAMS, AND/OR ASSETS
-LOSS OF MISSION
-LOSS OF LIFE

qas.q: what must authorized personnel do before permitting another individual to enter a sensitive compartmented information facility? (SCIF)
qas.a: only expressed authorized government owned PEDs.

qas.q: which of the following is true of security classification guides?
qas.a: all of these

qas.q: which of the following is NOT a potential consequence of using removable media unsafely in a sensitive Compartmented information facility? (SCIF)
qas.a: ANSWER IS NOT -by accepting cookies, you authorize websites to store your personal data on a web server.

qas.q: what portable electronic device (PEDs) are permitted in SCIF?
qas.a: investigate the destination by using the preview feature to see where the link actually leads.

qas.q: what is the response to an incident such as opening an uncontrolled DVD on a computer in a SCIF?
qas.a: you must update and validate your information.

qas.q: which of the following statement is true of cookies?
qas.a: they can be part of a distributed denial of service (DDoS) attack.

qas.q: what action should you take with a compressed uniform resource locator (URL) on a website known to you
qas.a: the physical security of the device.

qas.q: what is a common indicator of phishing attempt?
qas.a:
ANSWER IS NOT -
Digital video interface (DVI)

qas.q: which of the following is true of internet hoaxes?
qas.a: it may occur at any time without your knowledge or consent.

qas.q: which of the following is a concern when using our government issued laptop in public?
qas.a: enable automatic screen locking after a period of inactivity.

qas.q: which of the following is NOT a permitted way to connect to personally-owned monitor to your goverment furnished equipment? (GFE)?
qas.a: ITS NEVER OK!!!

qas.q: Which of the following best describes the conditions under which mobile devices and applications can track your location?
qas.a: Linda encrypts all of the sensitive data on her government-issued mobile device.

qas.q: how can you protect data on your mobile computing and portable electronics device (PEDS)?
qas.a: they can become an attack vector to other devices on your home network.
